The 3.4 million individual members are served through the 1,619 offices located in 77 provinces and 129 cities all over the country. They are also served by 70 ATM branches of the Network. The 772 cooperatives have consolidated assets of more than P101 Billion (US.2 billion).

name: Network Consolidated Cooperative Bank (NCCB)
description: NCCB is a consolidation of 6 Cooperative Banks. The Coop Bank operates nationwide through its 7 strategically located branches and engages primarily in financial products like deposits and loans.

name: One Cooperative Bank (One CB)
description: One Cooperative Bank (One CB) is the largest cooperative bank in the Philippines. It is the result of the consolidation of three (3) cooperative banks, namely Metro South Cooperative Bank (MSCB), Consolidated Cooperative Bank (CCB), and Bataan Cooperative Bank (BCB). Their direct members are primary cooperatives and individuals. They provide full banking, savings, financial and credit services for cooperatives and their members.

name: One-Cooperative Federation
description: OCF members are primary cooperatives with credit operations. They provide numerous training to its’ members, both mandatory and specialised, to develop skills and knowledge in managing the cooperatives. Their business focus is on providing lower interest rates on loans for member’ coops monetary requirements, consultancy, and brokering of their members’ products and services.

name: Philippine Family Farmers Agriculture Fishery Forestry Cooperatives Federation (AgriCOOPh)
description: AgriCOOPh Federation provides full-value chain services to agri-fishery-forestry cooperatives in the Philippines, enabling them to achieve sustainable growth and competitiveness in the agriculture and rural development sectors. These services include capacity building, financial assistance, market linkage support, technology adoption, and policy advocacy to strengthen the operations of member cooperatives. AgriCOOPh Federation also facilitates access to farm inputs, modern equipment, post-harvest facilities, and export opportunities, empowering cooperatives to maximize productivity and profitability while promoting environmental stewardship and social equity. By fostering collaboration and innovation across the value chain, AgriCOOPh Federation plays a vital role in improving the livelihoods of small-holder family farmers, fisherfolk, and rural communities in the Philippines. They have 60 direct members representing 900,000 individual members.

name: Providers Multipurpose Cooperative
description: Originally a savings and credit cooperative that transformed into a multi-service cooperative about 10 years ago. They provide the following services: lending, deposit, retail, medical, insurance, funeral, etcetera.

name: Union of Legitimate Service Contracting Cooperatives (ULSCC)
description: ULSCC is a union comprised of 37 labor service cooperatives in the Philippines. They are the government’s partner in job generation, job facilitation, deployment of workers, advancement of policies and advocacies for the labor sector, and job security by contributing to the continuous training and development of workers’ skills required by industry.

name: Victo National Co-operative Federation And Development Center (VICTO National)
description: VICTO National was founded in 1970 by Canadian missionaries called the Scarboro Fathers who started a parish program in Southern Leyte called “Saving Souls the Credit Union Way”. Its core service is education and training. It offers the widest variety of courses in the country, ranging from mandatory seminars required by the Cooperative Development Authority (CDA) to technical, motivational and specialized trainings.

name: Karachi Co-operative Housing Societies Union Ltd. (KCHS Union Ltd.)
description: We are a housing cooperative that provides low cost housing by acquiring land from the government. The Karachi Cooperative Housing Societies Union Ltd, established in the year 1949 under the Cooperative Societies Act 1925, has been playing a vital role in furtherance and development of a township on the most modern lines under the Cooperative fold, which has been acknowledged by various governments from time to time, commended by the foreign dignitaries and admired by the National and International conference.

name: National Association Of Co-operative Savings And Credit Unions (NACSCU)
description: NACSCU is a cooperative of credit unions. The aim of NACSCU activity is to ensure the financial stability of the credit unions, and in particular to grant financial support to the credit unions from the resources of the stabilisation fund, and to exercise control over the credit unions to ensure the security of the savings accumulated in them and the compliance of the activities of the creditunions with the provisions of the law.

name: National Auditing Union of Cooperatives (NAUWC)
description: NAUWC is a voluntary and self-governing organisation, which associates labour cooperatives and all othercooperative organisations in Poland. NAUWC runs statutory and legal activities in the following fields: Auditing financial statements of cooperatives; Providing consultancy as well as financial, legal, and self-governing trainings for members of supervisory boards, management boards and staff of workers’ cooperatives and cooperative organisations; Training personnel for cooperatives, scientific & technical cooperation and publishing activities; Archiving documents of liquidated cooperatives and cooperative organisations; Providing economic integration and promotion of workers’ cooperatives in Poland and abroad; Functioning of mutual aid funds, including the Cooperative Development Fund and the Cooperative Credit Guarantee Fund; Awarding state, branch and regional prizes to workers’ cooperatives, cooperative organisations and their members for activities in the fields of self-government and economy; Promoting international cooperative principles and the Polish workers’ cooperatives traditions; Representing workers’ cooperatives circles in Poland and abroad. NAUWC represented 6,200 individual members in 2011.

name: National Cooperative Council - NCC
description: NCC is the apex organization of the cooperatives of all sectors in Poland. It represents Polish cooperative movement in the country and abroad, initiates and gives its opinion on legislation concerning cooperatives, conducts training and educational activities, consultancy and advising, auditing, conducts cooperative development projects.

name: National Supervision Union Of Spolem Consumer Co-operatives
description: KZRSS is a national federation of consumer co-peratives. The aim of the Association is to assist and facilitate the realization of their affiliated cooperatives statutory tasks and inspire cooperation cooperatives for their development and integration. The tasks of the Association include: Provide training and advisory services in the areas of legal, economic and financial, tax and accounting assistance in selecting the most effective organizational and economic forms and methods of co-operative management training activities and the publishing and promotion of cultural and educational activities representing the interests of cooperatives affiliated to bodies of state administration and local self-government bodies representing the affiliated cooperatives abroad and in international co-operative movement initiate and develop cooperation between cooperatives and cooperation with research institutions development of information and analysis for the affiliated cooperatives organizing audit accounts affiliated cooperatives on the basis of specific regulations providing assistance to affiliated cooperatives in social activities There are around 49,000 individual members in consumer co-operatives. This number is variable as a consumers cooperative is an organization open and anyone who meets the requirements of the Statute of the cooperative pays a buy-in and participation may be admitted as members.

name: Banco Cooperativo de Puerto Rico (Bancoop)
description: Bancoop is the central financial institution of the co-operative movement in Puerto Rico.

name: Cooperativa de Ahorro y Crédito Vega Alta (VEGACOOP)
description: VEGACOOP is a financial institution.

name: Cooperativa de Ahorro y Crédito de Arecibo (COOPACA)
description: COOPACA is a communal savings and credit cooperative. They provide financial and insurance services to members.

name: Cooperativa de Seguros Múltiples de Puerto Rico
description: Cooperativa de Seguros Multiples of Puerto Rico is an insurance co-operative, to provide extraordinary protection services to individuals and organizations. It belongs to about 200 credit unions (and other types).

name: Instituto de Cooperativismo (ICOOP) Universidad de Puerto Rico
description: The Institute of Cooperativism of the University of Puerto Rico is a permanent center for cooperative education. Mission: To provide cooperative education and services to promote fair and equitable socioeconomic development in the country. To develop, therefore, research, higher education and the creation of cooperatives or community-based organizations that practice the values of the cooperative philosophy and the solidarity economy. The Institute of Cooperativism offers services to the university community, to the Cooperative Movement and to the community in general through academic, extension and cooperative research and development programs.

name: Liga de Cooperativas de Puerto Rico (LIGACOOP)
description: La Liga de Cooperativas de Puerto Rico es el organismo representativo de más alto nivel en Puerto Rico. Fue creada el 3 de junio de 1948 para representar al Movimiento Cooperativo Puertorriqueño.

name: Cooperative Work Agency (CWA)
description: CWA was launched towards the end of 2017, following approval of the Law on Cooperative passed in November that same year. It was established to fulfil two major roles: 1. A legal role/function covering the registration, financial and administrative audit of cooperatives, and other regulatory procedures such as liquidation of coops and investigation into coop financial/administrative issues. This role is currently active. 2. Promoting and supporting the development of coops through education/awareness raising (to be institutionalied through the Cooperative Development Institute) as well as facilitating coop access to funding and capacity building in the area of business planning (to be institutionalised through the Cooeprative Development Fund). These will take some time to fully operationalize. The strategic objectives of CWA as spelled out in the 2017-22 Strategy are as follows: 1. Build an institutional environment that enables the cooperative movement to grow and develop. 2. Improving the financial and organizational performance of cooepratives. 3. Expanding cooperative sectors/areas to include new tapes/modalities of cooperation.

name: Economic and Social Development Center of Palestine (ESDC)
description: ESDC is a Palestinian results and rights based development-oriented NGO established in 2003 and, as a learning organization, has accumulated vast experience in the both development and humanitarian cooperation. ESDC works in developing and building the capacities of cooperatives and the cooperative sector. They also develop their capacities in natural resources management, agriculture, food security, and social economy.

name: General Cooperative Union (GUCOOP.PAL)
description: They consist of 5 sectoral unions in Palestine, namely Agricultural Cooperative Union, the Housing Cooperative Union, the Cooperative Credit and Savings Union, the Consumer Cooperative Union, and the Crafts Cooperative Union. Their purposes include representing the Palestinian cooperative movement, providing advisory, legal and technical services, spreading awareness, helping create coops and sectoral cooperative unions, information for coopeatives, funding for cooperative projects, resolving disputes, and proposing rules & regulations in cooperation and coordination with the Cooperative Work Authority.

name: Kalandia Camp Women's Handicraft Cooperative (KCWHC)
description: KCWHC is one of the first cooperatives established in Palestine to provide aid and support for Palestinians across refugee camps in the Middle East region. It is also the 2nd registered coop in Palestine to be owned by women. KCWHC has been advocating for marginalised Palestinian women and families in the Kalandia refugee camp, as well as neighbouring camps and remote villages in the middle areas of Palestine and part of Gaza Strip in an effort to improve the social and economic situation of these families. One of the aims is to raise cultural, social, education and economic standards of women in refugee camps, villages and cities, through general services and vocational training courses so that they become activately capable and participate in serving their families and community. They also have a services division providing sponsorship schemes, literacy classes, health courses, first aid education, university student funds, kindergarten catering, summer school and general lectures. The production unit consists of industrial clothes sewing factory, production of Palestinian embroidery, handicrafts, kitting production, dried herbs and other agri-food processing, and quilting industry factory. Lastly, the Humanitarian and Services Unit includes children’s sponsorship, literacy, health, civic education courses, first aid education kindergarten, summer school and camps, general awareness lectures on elections, labor law, and coop law. They have many income-generating projects...

name: Palestinian Agriculture Cooperative Union (PACU)
description: PACU representing all of the 79 agriculture co-operatives in Palestine. They manage, fund and monitor the collective economic, social, cultural and environmental goals of its members. PACU promotes co-operatives run by local farmers in order to better represent their interests on a national and international level. They are working to organise the Palestinian agriculture sector in order to enhance their production performance and increase the productivity of the agriculture sector.

name: Union of Housing Cooperatives in Palestine (PUHC)
description: PUHC seeks to support the collective efforts of individuals and/or groups from the middle to low-income strata to obtain decent housing. Its members are voluntarily linked as co-operative housing associations. The Federation aspires to contribute to guaranteeing the right of Palestinians to live decently and to strengthen the ability of Palestinians to maintain their land by enabling them to build on all Palestinian territories.

name: CASES - Cooperativa António Sérgio para a Economia Social, CIPRL
description: CASES is a common interest co-operative whose members are both the government of Portugal and social economy organisations (co-op confederations, mutuals and entities that represent families of social economy). CASES is not a federation, nor is it an apex -- but a co-operative of entities (public and non-profit private, and not of individual persons). It probably most resembles the structure of a shared-service co-operative. CASES promotes the strengthening of the social economy sector, deepening cooperation between the State and the organizations that integrate it, with a view to stimulating its potential at the service of the country's socio-economic development, as well as the pursuit of policies in the area of volunteering. It engages in research, training, encourages public-private partnerships, provides input on public regulation of co-operatives (legislation, tax, etc) and social economy, represents the social economy. It also has a supervisory role over co-operatives (a bit like a registrar in that it issues legal certification of co-ops and can recommend dissolution of co-ops). CASES has 6 members.

name: Confecoop - Confederação Cooperativa Portuguesa, CCRL
description: CONFECOOP is the Confederation for the co-operative movement in Portugal, not including the agricultural movement and Banking / Credit Unions. CONFECOOP represents 10 of the 12 cooperative sectors in Portugal. The CONFECOOP´s members are national federation and cooperative organisations from all sectors of the economy, namely handicraft, consumer, culture, fisheries, health, housing, insurance, industry & services, social and solidarity sector. They represent 3 big Nacional Federations (FENACERCI , FENACHE and FENACOOP) and several important cooperatives from different sectors.

name: Confederación Paraguaya De Cooperativas CONPACOOP Ltda.
description: La Confederación Paraguaya de Cooperativas – CONPACOOP Ltda., constituida el 15 de octubre de 1991, es una cooperativa de tercer grado, principal órgano de representación y defensa gremial del movimiento cooperativo paraguayo. Sus entidades asociadas son las cooperativas de segundo grado, tanto Centrales como Federaciones. Acorde a sus funciones de carácter gremial se constituye en centro de discusión y análisis de temas que afectan a todo el sector cooperativo del país, en los aspectos legales, jurídicos, tributarios, educativos.

name: Confederación de Cooperativas Rurales del Paraguay (CONCOPAR Ltda.)
description: CONCOPAR is a co-operative Union of the third degree, with non-profit character and does not distribute surplus among its members. It is intended, to exercise trade union representation, at the country level as well as internationally for the affiliated entities, strive for the observance of the co-operative principles doctrine. Encourage the creation and strengthening of new cooperatives and organizations of second degree, preferably in agricultural, forestry and rural environment.

name: Cooperativa Multiactiva de Ahorro, Crédito, Consumo, Producción y Servicios “8 de Marzo Limitada”
description: 8 de Marzo Limitada is capitalized primarily with the social contribution of its individual members. Its headquarters or head office is located in Lambare, adjacent to the capital Asunción, so its strategic location is part of the metropolitan area, where the economic and social operational mass of the whole country is largely concentrated. It currently has 23 branches with their own headquarters that underpin its growth and projection to the entire national territory. Its business strength allows it to provide its members with a wide range of quality financial and social services, practicing cooperative values.

name: Cooperativa Universitaria Ltda.
description: La Cooperativa Universitaria es una cooperativa primaria fundada el 1973. La CU desarrolla actividades financieras y sociales, siendo líder del sector cooperativo, cuenta con talento humano competente y servicios orientados a mejorar la calidad de vida de sus socios. Representa a más de 100 mil miembros individuales activos. Su Misión es conectarse con las personas, generando soluciones con servicios financieros adecuados y sustentables, a través del cooperativismo, para beneficio de los socios y la comunidad.

name: Federación de Cooperativas Multiactivas del Paraguay (FECOMULP LTDA.)
description: La Federación de Cooperativas Multiactivas del Paraguay – FECOMULP Ltda., es una federación cuya membresía integran 29 cooperativas de primer grado, cuyos fines principales son la defensa gremial y la educación de sus cooperativas afiliadas.

name: Federación de Cooperativas de Ahorro y Crédito Ltda. (FECOAC)
description: FECOAC is a second-grade organization, founded with the aim of providing its member cooperatives education, advisory, technical assistance and union defense services. Also, among its objectives is to act as a link between its members and public and private organizations, as well as with international organizations linked to the solidarity sector. 76 member societies (cooperatives) 522,743 individual members

name: Federación de Cooperativas de Producción  (FECOPROD)
description: FECOPROD is a second-tier union made up of 34 production cooperatives. Its’ obectives are proactive union defense, training and technical support of primary cooperatives, the member cooperatives carry out the production, transformation and commercialization in their bases, the FECOPROD carries out the technical and political procedures to facilitate these actions. Its main purpose is institutional strengthening, establishing mechanisms for the defense of the economic and social interests of its associates.

name: Federación de Cooperativas del Paraguay (FECOPAR LTDA.)
description: La Federación es una asociación autónoma y voluntaria de Cooperativas, que se unen sobre la base del esfuerzo propio y la ayuda mutua, para organizar una Sociedad Cooperativa de Segundo Grado, sin fines de lucro, con el propósito de satisfacer necesidades educativas, gremiales, luchar por la equidad de género, la preservación del medio ambiente y todo aquello que tienda al engrandecimiento y consolidación del movimiento cooperativo paraguayo.

name: National Union of Consumer Co-operatives (CENTROCOOP)
description: In charge of representing consumer co-operatives at the national level

name: Romanian National Union of Handicraft and Production Cooperatives Association - UCECOM
description: UCECOM is the representing organization of the handicraft co-operatives, at the national and international level. UCECOM was founded ”to assure the representation and promotion of the economic, social and cultural interests of the cooperators and associated members in their relations with the public administration, State authorities and other physical and/or legal persons, public or private, with similar international bodies, as well as to sustain and promote the co-operative principles”.

name: General cooperative alliance for agriculture and rural development - Belgrade
description: They are a cooperative union that inherited the Cooperative Federation of Yugoslavia, who was a previous member of the ICA. In 2016, they were reorganised and changed the name of the association. Their members are agriculture cooperatives from Serbia. They promote cooperatives, participate in fairs, cooperative audits, educate farmers, make business plans for cooperatives, and cooperate with agriculture institutes. They have 25 cooperatives and associaions in their membership. They cooperate with the cooperative unions of Northern Macedonia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Croatia and Slovenia. 19 cooperatives have 257 members (individual persons). Our 6 associations have 108 cooperatives with 1324 members (individual persons). A total of 1581 members of cooperatives ( individual persons).

name: Central Union of Consumer Societies of the Russian Federation (Centrosoyuz of the Russian Federation)
description: Voluntary association of the consumer societies in Russia, non-profit organization. Their tasks are: Development of the consumer co-operative system, protection of democratic basics and values of the cooperative movement; Realization of innovative business projects aiming to the foundation of modern infrastructure in different spheres of the economy; Coordination of consumer societies and unions and representing their interests in administrative and municipal authorities and international organizations; and To provide protection of property rights and other rights of consumer societies and their members. In 2011, they represented 4 million individual members.

name: Moscow Regional Union of Consumer Societies
description: A regional union of consumer societies.

name: National Cooperatives Confederation of Rwanda (NCCR)
description: 800x600 Normal 0 false false false EN-GB X-NONE X-NONE MicrosoftInternetExplorer4 st1:*{behavior:url(#ieooui) } /* Style Definitions */ table.MsoNormalTable {mso-style-name:"Table Normal"; mso-tstyle-rowband-size:0; mso-tstyle-colband-size:0; mso-style-noshow:yes; mso-style-priority:99; mso-style-parent:""; mso-padding-alt:0cm 5.4pt 0cm 5.4pt; mso-para-margin:0cm; mso-para-margin-bottom:.0001pt; mso-pagination:widow-orphan; font-size:10.0pt; font-family:"Times New Roman","serif";} NCCR is the apex organization for co-operatives in Rwanda. They represent, advocate for, and do lobbying on behalf of their federation members and co-operative unions that their members represent. They also assist in development of co-operatives and their members.

name: Coompanion Sverige Ekonomisk förening
description: Coompanion Sverige represents 25 regional and county co-operative development centres. They promote new co-operative enterprise; engage in advocacy; and provide technical assistance.

name: HSB Riksförbund (Federation of Cooperative Housing)
description: HSB is Sweden’s biggest cooperative housing organization, focusing on providing good and functional living. The revenue generated by HSB is invested back into the business. Good Living is both our history and passion. HSB started in 1923 as a cooperative organisation to provide better housing conditions and create new ideas that really improve domestic life. HSB in figures: 674 000 members 120 000 HSB home savers 340 000 cooperative apartments 4 100 housing associations 25 regional HSB associations

name: Kooperativa Förbundet (KF) (the Swedish Co-operative Union)
description: KF is a federation of consumer co-operatives in Sweden and a retail group, with groceries as its core business. Coop is available in over 700 locations nationwide and is the only cooperative grocery store chain in Sweden. As of 2015, KF is a federation of 31 consumer co-operatives with more than 3.4 million members and a retail group with a net worth (ex. VAT) of SEK 35 billion. The grocery retail group, Coop, stands for around 92 per cent of the sale with store brands like Stora Coop, Coop and Lilla Coop. KF accounts for approximately 44 percent of consumer cooperative retail sales in Sweden. The other part is run by local or regional retail cooperatives, even though procurement, logistics and store brands are integrated. Together, KF and the consumer co-operatives have a market share of 20.5 per cent of the Swedish grocery retail trade (2014). In 2015, they had 3 425 632 individual members.

name: Riksbyggen (co-operative Housing Union)
description: Riksbyggen, the Co-operative housing association, is owned by the trade unions of the construction sector, tenant ownership housing co-operatives and other national and local cooperative organisations.

name: Singapore National Co-operative Federation Ltd. (SNCF)
description: The Singapore National Co-operative Federation (SNCF) is the apex body of Singapore’s Co-operative Movement, and secretariat of the Central Co-operative Fund (CCF). Formed in 1980 with the aim of championing Singapore’s Co-operative Movement, the apex body represents 99% of co-operative members in Singapore through its affiliated co-operatives. Our comprehensive programmes help co-operatives strengthen their enterprises so they can better serve their members, the broader community and to transform the national economy.

name: Somali Union Co-operative Movement (UDHIS)
description: UDHIS is the umbrella organisation of the Somalian co-operative movement. The members are the six national sectoral unions, each of which have their own district unions. The movement in Somalia produces mostly sesame, banana, leather, seafood, maize and livestock.

name: Federación de Asociaciones Cooperativas de Ahorro y Crédito de El Salvador de R.L. (FEDECACES)
description: La FEDERACION DE ASOCIACIONES COOPERATIVAS DE AHORRO Y CREDITO DE EL SALVADOR DE R.L fue fundada el 11 de junio de 1966, con 52 años de edad, es una asociación cooperativa de integración, de segundo piso, sin fines de lucro y utilidad pública como entidad de desarrollo y representación gremial. Actor como sociedad civil y propiedad asociativa, con capital 100% de Salvadoreños de 30 Asociaciones Cooperativas de Ahorro y Crédito integradas y con una membresía de más de 225,000 asociados y más de un millón de usuarios. Fiscalizada por el INSAFOCOOP (Instituto Salvadoreño de Fomento Cooperativo). Tiene personalidad jurídica desde el 25 de abril de 1972 y domicilio legal en San Salvador, Departamento de San Salvador, El Salvador.

name: Eswatini Multipurpose Cooperative Union (ESWAMCU)
description: ESWAFCU is an apex body representing all farming cooperatives in Eswatini (Swaziland) to improve access to inputs, equipment, markets and technical information.

name: National Cooperatives Federation of Eswatini (NCFE)
Association of Asian Confederations of Credit Unions (ACCU)
description: ACCU is a regional member based organization of credit unions and cooperatives in Asia. ACCU plays a vital role in regional credit union development, to make credit unions more relevant community-based financial institutions. In 2021, ACCU represents 50.29 million individual members of 33,820 credit unions in 23 countries in Asia.

name: The Co-operative League of Thailand (CLT)
description: The Cooperative League of Thailand (CLT) is the national apex body of the Cooperative Movement in the country. Their members are co-operatives. The CLT plays a major role in the area of cooperation, promotion and development of the cooperatives in Thailand to help and promote sustainable development of Thai cooperatives. Currently, there are 7 types of cooperatives in primary levels; Agricultural Cooperatives, Land Settlement Cooperatives, Fishery Cooperatives, Saving and Credit Cooperatives, Credit Union Cooperatives, Service Cooperatives, and Consumer Cooperatives. Total number of cooperatives are 7,741 cooperatives with its individual members of 11,433,400 households. There are five bureaus serving the CLT activities; Office of the Director, Cooperative Training Institute, CLT Coop Training Centre, Member Relation Department, Cooperative Business Department, and Legal Consult and HRM Department.

name: Central Union Of The Agricultural Credit Cooperatives Of Türkiye (ACC)
description: Central Union of the Agricultural Credit Cooperatives of Türkiye meets all sorts of agricultural input needs and credit requirements of its members.

name: National Cooperative Union of Turkiye (TURKIYEKOOP)
description: Represents the cooperative sector in official and private studies on cooperatives. Contributes to the reparation of survey plans for the movement and shares views with official institutions. Carries out labboying activities, studies on increasing cooperative awareness, monitors activity of the ICA and provides information on the development of the international cooperative movement to its’ members. Only national cooperative parent organisations are members.

name: The Central Union of Turkish Forestry Co-operatives (ORKOOP)
description: ORKOOP member unions work in forestry and produce agriculture products. They assist their members in production, evaluation and marketing of forestry products. They also assist in development funds, waste disposal, loans, utilities, facilities, machinery, instruments, equipment, legal rights, environmental protection, social security, research, and training.

name: Co-operative Bank Tanzania Plc
description: Co-operative Bank of Tanzania is a commercial bank whose majority of shareholder members are cooperatives. Their aims are to drive socio-economic transformation of their members/customers to empower and improve livelihoods through financial inclusion and digital innovation.

name: Moshi Co-operative University (MoCU)
description: The Moshi Co-operative University (MoCU) is among the oldest co-operative training institution in Africa, where it was first established as the Co-operative College Moshi in 1963 offering accredited programmes in co-operative accounting, finance, marketing, management, and rural development. It has turned out experts in accounting, finance, auditing, marketing, management and co-operative development employed in different institutions – both co-operative and non-co-operatives - from within and outside Tanzania. It grew from a college enrolling 150 students in the 1960s to 1500 students in the 1990s. It was transformed into the Moshi University College of Co-operative and Business Studies (MUCCoBS) in 2004, a constituent university college of the Sokoine University of Agriculture. During its time as a University College it was able to increase its programmes to eleven and expand its enrolment to over 3000 students. In September, 2014 the government elevated MUCCoBS to a full-fledged university by the name of the Moshi Co-operative University in short MoCU. Currently, the university has thirty three programmes ranging from certificates, diploma, undergraduate, and postgraduate prorammes at Masters and Doctoral levels. In addition to accredited programmes, the university also runs outreach programmes through its thirteen regional offices across the country to meet co-operative members’ and leaders’ short-term training requirements. It has an additional teach...

name: Tanzania Federation of Co-operatives Ltd. (TFC)
description: TFC is the apex organisation for the entire co-operative movement in Tanzania. They represented 1,380,000 individual members in 2021.

name: Ukranian Central Union of Consumer Societies (UKRKOOPSPILKA)
description: The system of consumer co-operation of Ukraine is united by 23 associations of consumer associations from each region of the country in the Ukrainian Central Union of Consumer Societies (Ukrkoopspilka).

name: Uganda Co-operative Alliance Ltd. (UCA)
description: UCA is an umbrella organisation of cooperative organisations registered in 1961 with the aim of promoting the economic and social interests of cooperatives in Uganda. It was formed for the purposes of promoting, advocating and building the capacities of all types of co-operatives in the country (primary societies, district and national unions).

name: Uganda Health Partners Cooperative Limited (UHPC)
description: Uganda Health Partners Cooperative (UHPC) is a newly registered Unique worker cooperative. Their vision is to create communities that have equitable access to quality and affordable health outcomes through health cooperatives. To achieve this vision, UHPC will train trainers, collaborate with the government, medical bureaus and other partners. Their collaboration effort has established a network of locally-led organisations, developed tools for capacity building and management, and scaled up the health cooepraive model.

name: America’s Credit Unions
description: CUNA is the federation representing the credit unions in the United States. In 2011, they had 96,000,000 individual members. CUNA provides member credit unions with trade association services, such as lobbying, regulatory advocacy, professional development, and professional services management. CUNA employs approximately 70 people in its Washington, D.C. office as well as several hundred people in its Madison, Wisconsin office.

name: CoBank, ACB
description: CoBank is a national cooperative bank serving vital industries across rural America. The bank provides loans, leases, export financing and other financial services to agribusinesses and rural power, water and communications providers in all 50 states. CoBank is a member of the Farm Credit System, a nationwide network of banks and retail lending associations chartered to support the borrowing needs of U.S. agriculture and the nation's rural economy. In addition to serving its direct retail borrowers, the bank also provides wholesale loans and other financial services to affiliated Farm Credit associations serving approximately 75,000 farmers, ranchers and other rural borrowers in 23 states around the country. Headquartered outside Denver, Colorado, CoBank serves customers from regional banking centers across the U.S. and also maintains an international representative office in Singapore.

name: National Co+op Grocers (NCG)
description: Business services and purchasing cooperative serving retail food co-ops operating in the United States.

name: National Cooperative Bank (NCB)
description: NCB provides comprehensive banking services to cooperatives and other member-owned organizations throughout the country. What makes NCB unique is that the bank was created to address the financial needs of an underserved market niche – people who join together cooperatively to meet personal, social or business needs, especially in low-income communities. In 2012, NCB's earnings were USD 13.7 million, its loans were USD 1.4 billion. They publish the NCB Co-op 100, a ranking of the top co-ops in the United States.

name: National Cooperative Business Association CLUSA International (NCBA CLUSA)
description: The National Cooperative Business Association CLUSA International (NCBA CLUSA) is the apex association for cooperatives in the U.S. NCBA CLUSA has worked for more than 100 years so that more people can use cooperatives to build a more inclusive economy. NCBA CLUSA achieves this vision through collaborative partnerships in development, advocacy, public awareness and thought leadership. Beyond domestic advocacy and public awareness, we have engaged in international development for nearly 70 years.

name: National Rural Electric Cooperative Association (NRECA)
description: NRECA is the national service organization for more than 900 not-for-profit rural electric cooperatives and public power districts providing retail electric service to more than 42 million consumers in 47 states and whose retail sales account for approximately 12 percent of total electricity sales in the United States. NRECA’s members include consumer-owned local distribution systems — the vast majority — and 66 generation and transmission (G&T) cooperatives that supply wholesale power to their distribution cooperative owner-members. Distribution and G&T cooperatives share an obligation to serve their members by providing safe, reliable and affordable electric service. NRECA membership includes other organizations formed by these local utilities: Generation and transmission cooperatives for power supply Statewide and regional trade and service associations Supply and manufacturing cooperatives Data processing cooperatives Employee credit unions

name: National Society Of Accountants For Co-operatives (NSAC)
description: NSAC is an associate of accountants which represents the needs of co-operative accounting, auditing and tax professionals. Co-operatives use their service to find accountants which specialize in co-operatives. NSAC provides accounting, tax and business education to co-operatives; provide networking and professional development activities for their member accountants; and support co-operative business interests in standard setting processes (legal and regulatory). NSAC’s membership is made up of 1,400 individuals (2009). Their income comes from membership subscriptions.

name: Confederación Uruguaya de Entidades Cooperativas (CUDECOOP)
description: Fundada en 1988, es la máxima entidad de representación nacional e internacional del Movimiento Cooperativo Uruguayo. Promueve su integración y articulación en todas sus dimensiones, contribuyendo a su desarrollo y fortalecimiento.

name: Cooperativas Nacionales Financieras Aliadas en Red (CONFIAR)
description: CONFIAR is a secondary consumer co-operative organization, currently made up of 3 co-operative unions. CONFIAR provides services such as: -Management of credit with reciprocal guarantees. -Representation of common and individual interests of its associates -Fixed income desk -Economy of scale is the next service their offer to their membership. It’s a fund created for their members. CONFIAR promotes, develops and facilitates economic activities and comprehensive training of their associates through the mutual provision of services of all kinds, such as technical, educational, economic, financial, advisory, administrative or commercial. www.confiaruruguay.coop

name: Cámara Uruguaya de Cooperativas de Ahorro y Crédito and Capitalización (CUCACC)
description: Somos una asociación gremial que defiende y representa institucionalmente a las cooperativas de ahorro y crédito de capitalización.

name: Federación de Cooperativas de Vivienda de Usuarios por Ahorro Previo (FECOVI)
description: FECOVI is a federation of housing cooperatives. Its objectives are to represent cooperatives as a trade union, promote the system nationally and internationally, carry out studies and proposals for improvement, and engage in dialogue with political, governmental and civil society actors to promote cooperativism in general, housing cooperativism in particular, and housing cooperativism through pre-savings, more specifically. Founded in 1984, when the process of return to democracy began in Uruguay, FECOVI is the heir of FENACOVI, founded in 1968 and outlawed during the coup d'état. FECOVI brings together 95% of the users' housing cooperatives by prior savings, enjoying one of the highest representation at the national level. In addition to representation, it is the main promoter of the system and provides non-profit support services to its member cooperatives, including technical advice on economic, social, legal and architectural matters, as well as ongoing training through regular courses and workshops.

name: Instituto Nacional del Cooperativismo (INACOOP)
description: INACOOP tiene como objetivo promover el desarrollo económico, social y cultural del sector cooperativo y su inserción en el desarrollo del país. La Ley 18.407 le asigna cometidos vinculados con el asesoramiento al Estado, la promoción de los principios y valores de la cooperación, la formulación y evaluación de planes de desarrollo cooperativo, el desarrollo de procesos asociativos, integradores y participativos entre las cooperativas, así como la coordinación e implementación de investigaciones, creando un sistema nacional de información de carácter público sobre el sector. Evalua la incidencia del cooperativismo en la economía y en la sociedad, está encargado de la comunicación pública de la temática cooperativa y del impulso, la investigación y propuestas para el desarrollo de procesos asociativos e integradores, considerando las diversas manifestaciones de la economía social y solidaria.

name: Saigon Union of Trading Co-operatives
description: Saigon Co.op operates the supermarket business. They distribute quality goods to cooperative members to consumers through multiple retail formats (supermarkets, hypermarkets, shopping mall, TV shopping, online, etc.) They also invest in import and export products and production factories. Many of the goods that they distribute to their members come from cooperatives. Saigon Co.op, by their negotiation power, provides the products (offered by suppliers in the market) to sell in the 26 cooperative member stores. In that way, they can get the products at lower cost since they cannot negotiate directly with big suppliers. They also support the members in training staff to operate the store if they require.

name: Vietnam Co-operative Alliance (VCA)
description: VCA is the apex organsation representing the entire co-operative movement in Vietnam.

name: Office of the Registrar of Cooperatives and Business Development Services (ORCBDS)
description: ORCBDS is a government department established to assist with rural economic development, and includes the department of NI-Vanuata Business. Currently the department dedicates most of its time to cooperative societies because the Cooperative Act mandates ORCBDS to assist and promote business activities for cooperative societies and its members. Their mission is to facilitate sustainable growth in co-operative business and to advance the interests of Vanuata citizens both domestically and internationally by: Promoting increased rural savings and investment through co-operative savings and loans societies Encouraging the establishment and diversification of Ni-Vanuatu business Supporting co-operative societies by providing advisory and business appraisal services

name: National Cooperatives Association of South Africa (NCASA)
description: A national federation of all forms and types of cooperatives. Their members are tertiary cooperatives (coop federations) and national secondary cooperatives.

name: South African National Apex Co-op (SANACO)
description: SANACO is the national apex of co-operatives in South Africa. Their aim is to advocate for and on behalf of co-operatives and their development. They assisted with the ILO and other groups to get new co-operative legislation passed based on ICA and ILO Recommendation 193.

name: Zimbabwe National Association Of Housing Co-operatives (ZINAHCO)
description: ZINAHCO is an apex body. It is non-profit. It works with low to non-income Zimbabwean people and currently has a membership of home-seekers. They facilitate access to loans and housing insurance.
